key_points: New $200-350 Christmas break fee outraged students, petitions launched
Camp Hall elevators frequently broken; bathrooms lack ventilation fans
McMahon Hall fire alarms go off constantly due to bad cooking
key_quotes: I have never seen this school this crowded, parking is full everywhere on campus
At least 1 of the 3 elevators are always broken at some point
UAB is nickel and diming people, especially with how the political climate is making international students afraid to leave

key_points: Not a party school — frat parties exist but nothing like UA or Auburn
Loneliness and social isolation are real, recurring complaints from students
400+ clubs available; students who join early report finding community quickly
key_quotes: Since transferring to UAB I haven't participated much because of this exact issue — finding people to go with
Students struggle with high rates of anxiety and depression due to loneliness and lack of community
UAB isn't a huge party school, there is definitely [stuff to do] but it depends on you

key_points: Sodexo food widely called 'slop' — students preferred food trucks that disappeared
Gluten-free and dietary restriction options are severely limited on campus
Hill dining hall overcrowded this year with no seats during peak hours
key_quotes: Also objectively better than Sodexo slop — it was the only real food on campus
I'm gluten free and can't eat literally anything else available, even mein bowl sushi
Hill was the most crowded I have ever seen it, nowhere to sit and the longest lines I've ever seen

key_points: Social isolation and loneliness flagged repeatedly as serious student welfare issues
UAB fired an employee for mental-health-related absences despite documented struggles
Students report anxiety barriers preventing participation in campus life
key_quotes: Students struggle with high rates of anxiety and depression due to loneliness, lack of community, and social isolation
Yes, my anxiety keeps me in the house
I was fired for calling in too many times due to my mental health, almost being hospitalized

key_points: Scholarship tiers are competitive; retaking ACT multiple times is common to move up
FAFSA refund delays frustrated students, especially transfers used to faster timelines
Employee tuition benefit exists but varies by entity (UAB vs Hospital vs UAHSF)
key_quotes: The 8k scholarship will not be enough to cover everything, especially if you are going on campus
I have never had a university hold a refund this long
UAHSF gets 12 hours per academic year — if you're full time, one semester. Better than nothing

key_points: Biomedical engineering and nursing programs are genuinely strong and well-regarded
Advisor errors can go undetected for years — students must verify their own GPS
Some weed-out courses like Orgo have reputations for difficulty; lecture not always recorded
key_quotes: Biomedical engineering is a phenomenal program here — great research, labs, mentorship, ABET accredited
Advisors across the board are usually not the best when it comes to understanding graduation requirements
The lectures aren't recorded, the slides alone aren't enough to study from, and the exams can be pretty difficult

key_points: Multiple petitions launched against admin decisions — Christmas fee, parking, commencement cancellation
Summer 2026 commencement cancelled due to arena construction with no alternate venue offered
At least one allegation of retaliation against a student who filed a civil rights complaint
key_quotes: This, along with the mass ticketing, has been a clear example of the most egregious money grabbing in this university's history
I'm shocked they didn't provide an alternate location — I graduated during the pandemic when they moved it to legion field
Last year they were pretty brutal towards a student with a disability that filed a federal civil rights complaint

key_points: Birmingham offers Railroad Park, bars, restaurants, and live music — more than campus alone
Campus green and new buildings impress visitors; described as peaceful and beautiful
Walking safety is fine in daylight but PhD students warned about late-night lab commutes
key_quotes: Downtown Birmingham has a lot of fun places — Railroad Park, Regions Field, lots of bars and restaurants
It really impressed me — so amazed by the beauty and peacefulness of the campus
It's completely safe if you are going to be walking in daylight. I did it for 3 years

key_points: Strong pipeline into UAB Hospital system for nursing and pre-med students
UAB Hospital hiring is competitive internally; being fired there can blacklist you from rehire
ICU nursing staff widely praised in YouTube comments for skill and professionalism
key_quotes: UAB is great for BME — you'll be fine, it's medical adjacent and has first-rate connections
As a hiring manager, you being fired is going to come up — most other departments won't hire you
I say this as an ICU RN at UAB: this state is awful for nurses, especially UAB — pay us more

key_points: Scholarship money is genuinely generous for high-stat students — full rides possible
Parking passes don't guarantee spots; students advise not buying one and parking free nearby
Out-of-state students consistently advised by peers to leverage UAB scholarships vs. pricier options
key_quotes: If you just park there without a permit it is still less money to pay tickets than to pay for the permit itself
I think I was the Blazer Scholarship at UAB — $10k per semester!
UAB is one of the best schools in the area — I love it and would suggest it, especially if money is tight

key_points: Commuter-heavy, urban, pre-med-dominant culture — not a traditional residential campus feel
Students who engage early with clubs find community; those who don't can feel invisible
Athletics dysfunction and admin friction create cynicism, but academic pride runs deep
key_quotes: Coming from someone that went to UA that's now going to UAB — go to UAB
UAB is amazing — making friends was super easy, but I started early and knocked on dorm doors with cookies
There's a lot of student culture — I have absolutely no regrets about choosing UAB over UA

key_points: **Administration:** Cancelled summer commencement with no backup venue; new fees added without notice
**Athletics:** Coaching scandal (Dilfer era), softball coach abuse allegations, and a stabbing at the football facility in one year
**Parking:** System is genuinely broken — passes sell out, spots aren't guaranteed, new ticket rules catch students off guard
key_quotes: This and the stabbing in a 6-month span? On top of athletics being at its collective low point in its 50-year history?
ADA compliance is terrible and they are fully aware but ignore it
UAB stopped withholding and matching for my 403b — I did not unenroll. They stopped and said they would not back-pay

key_points: Employee tuition benefit is a little-known path to a nearly free UAB degree
BME program rivals much pricier schools — ABET accredited with strong research access
Free parking exists near tennis courts and Ramsey High if you arrive early
key_quotes: My friend worked for the steam plant on campus and got free tuition — he graduated with his degree in electrical engineering
UAB has a first-rate biomed engineering program — you can't go wrong with that
Get there early — I got to campus at 7am and the G lots were empty
